title: malus communis
intro: Malus communis, also known as the Common Crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus communis include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Malus communis can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and infusions of the flowers, leaves, and bark, which can be used to treat various ailments.
